seo.title: AI Agents & Tool Use implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping AI Agents & Tool Use projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for transitioning AI agents from prototype to production. It focuses on reliability, cost control, and security within agentic workflows.

items.title: JSON Schema Validation
items.description: Verify that all tool definitions include strict JSON schemas with required fields and type constraints to prevent LLM hallucination of arguments.

items.title: Standardized Error Payloads
items.description: Implement a standard format for tool error messages that provide the LLM with actionable feedback on how to fix incorrect parameters.

items.title: Description Uniqueness Check
items.description: Ensure no two tools have overlapping semantic descriptions to prevent the model from selecting the wrong tool for a given task.

items.title: Tool Versioning
items.description: Implement a versioning system for tool schemas to allow for rolling updates without breaking active long-running agent sessions.

items.title: Dry-Run Mode
items.description: Create a flag for each tool to simulate execution, returning a mock response to test agent planning without side effects.

items.title: Hard Iteration Limits
items.description: Set a maximum number of steps (e.g., 10-15) for the agent loop to prevent infinite loops and runaway costs.

items.title: Duplicate Call Detection
items.description: Implement logic to detect and stop the agent if it calls the same tool with the same arguments multiple times in a single session.

items.title: Stuck-Loop Detection
items.description: Monitor for repetitive thought patterns in the LLM output and trigger a forced context reset or human intervention if detected.

items.title: Context Window Management
items.description: Implement a strategy for pruning or summarizing history when the agent's scratchpad approaches the model's token limit.

items.title: Graceful Exit Handlers
items.description: Define explicit exit conditions for the agent, including when a goal is unreachable or requires unavailable tools.

items.title: Trace ID Propagation
items.description: Pass a unique trace ID through every step of the agent loop to link LLM prompts, tool calls, and final outputs in logs.

items.title: Token Usage Tracking
items.description: Log the cumulative token count and cost for every agent run to identify high-cost workflows and optimize prompt length.

items.title: Tool Latency Monitoring
items.description: Record the execution time of each tool call to identify bottlenecks in the agent's external integrations.

items.title: Raw Prompt Persistence
items.description: Store the exact prompt sent to the LLM at each step (including few-shot examples) for post-incident debugging.

items.title: Step-by-Step UI Visualization
items.description: Provide a real-time log or graph view for developers to see the agent's current 'thought' process and tool outputs.

items.title: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Triggers
items.description: Configure specific tools (e.g., payments, deletions) to require manual approval before the agent can execute them.

items.title: Sandboxed Tool Execution
items.description: Run tools that execute code or shell commands in isolated containers with restricted network and filesystem access.

items.title: Least-Privilege API Keys
items.description: Ensure the API keys used by the agent have the minimum necessary scopes required for the tools provided.

items.title: Input Sanitization
items.description: Validate and sanitize LLM-generated tool arguments to prevent SQL injection or prompt injection via tool inputs.

items.title: PII Scrubbing
items.description: Implement a middleware layer to detect and mask Personally Identifiable Information in agent logs and tool payloads.

items.title: Shared State Schema
items.description: Define a strict schema for the shared memory or state object used when handing off tasks between different specialized agents.

items.title: Handoff Logic Validation
items.description: Verify that the 'Router' agent has clear criteria for when to delegate a task to a sub-agent vs. handling it directly.

items.title: Conflict Resolution Rules
items.description: Establish priority levels for agents that may attempt to access or modify the same resource simultaneously.

items.title: Deadlock Monitoring
items.description: Implement checks to detect if two agents are waiting for each other's output before proceeding.

items.title: Agent-to-Agent Auth
items.description: Verify that handoffs between agents are authenticated and cannot be triggered by external spoofed requests.

items.title: Tool Result Caching
items.description: Implement a TTL-based cache for deterministic tool outputs to reduce redundant LLM processing and external API calls.

items.title: Parallel Tool Execution
items.description: Configure the agent to execute multiple independent tool calls in parallel when the LLM identifies several needed actions.

items.title: Tiered Model Routing
items.description: Use a smaller, cheaper model for simple tool selection and reserve larger models for complex planning and synthesis.

items.title: Streaming Tool Outputs
items.description: Ensure tool outputs are streamed to the agent's context as they finish to improve perceived user responsiveness.

items.title: Cost Quotas
items.description: Set hard daily or per-user budget limits that automatically disable agent execution when exceeded.
